title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
review: A masterfully told story of a legendary figure recounting his extraordinary life. Rothfuss weaves a tale of magic, music, and adventure that feels both epic and intimately personal. The prose is gorgeous and the world-building is some of the best in modern fantasy.

title: The Wise Man's Fear
author: Patrick Rothfuss
review: Even longer and more sprawling than the first, but equally captivating. Kvothe's time with the Adem and the Maer are highlights. I could happily live in this world forever.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
review: A brilliant, optimistic sci-fi adventure. The problem-solving is endlessly satisfying, the science is fascinating, and the unexpected friendship at its core is genuinely moving. Probably the most fun I've had reading a book in years.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
review: A dense, rewarding masterpiece of ecological and political science fiction. The world-building is unmatched. Took me a while to get into but by the halfway point I couldn't put it down.

title: Dune Messiah
author: Frank Herbert
review: A darker, more philosophical follow-up. Paul's tragedy hits hard. Deliberately subverts heroism in a way that makes the first book richer in retrospect.
